target.name: Dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indoline 4a
target.motivation: The target dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indoline 4a is a core structure found in rare natural products like phalarine. The authors aim to develop a reductive cyclization strategy to access this scaffold, complementing existing oxidative methods and addressing limitations such as the need for nitrogen protection.

prior_syntheses.key_strategy: Oxidative coupling of N-acylated indoles with phenols to form benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines.
Oxidative coupling of N-acylated indoles with phenols as a key step in the total synthesis of phalarine.
Intramolecular aminohydroxylation of stilbenes (hypervalent iodine-mediated) in the synthesis of phalarine.

success_criteria.free_text: Efficient access to dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indolines and benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines under mild conditions, bypassing the need for nitrogen protection and providing a unified strategy for both product classes.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-N bond and C-O bond formation
key_disconnections.reasoning: The authors hypothesized that a TiCl3-mediated reductive cyclization of ortho-nitro-substituted styrene or stilbene derivatives could provide a unified approach to the target structures. This would involve partial reduction of the nitro group to a nitroso intermediate, followed by 5-center-6π-electrocyclization to a nitrone, and subsequent intramolecular trapping by a tethered hydroxy or phenol group, then reduction of the hydroxylamine.

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: This pathway would lead to regioisomeric products (e.g., 2,3,3-trisubstituted indolenines or furo[2,3-b]indolines), which are not the desired [3,2-b] fused products. Precise control of reaction conditions is critical to override these rearrangement pathways.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The strategy aims to leverage the intrinsic reactivity of the nitro group, avoiding the multi-step sequence of nitro reduction and N-protection required by previous oxidative methods. The TiCl3-mediated reduction is inspired by the authors' prior success with Cadogan-Sundberg reactions, suggesting its potential for controlled reductive cyclizations.

thought.background: Previous studies showed aqueous TiCl3 efficiently promotes Cadogan-Sundberg reactions at room temperature, validated in total syntheses of complex indole alkaloids. Existing methods for furoindolines often require nitrogen protection and lack a unified approach for both dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indolines and benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines.
thought.gap: Can a reductive cyclization strategy, specifically TiCl3-mediated, bypass N-protection and provide a unified entry to both [3,2-b]-fused products from ortho-nitro-substituted styrene or stilbene derivatives?
thought.decision: To achieve a unified, protection-free synthesis of [3,2-b]-fused furoindolines, we will investigate TiCl3-mediated reductive cyclization of ortho-nitroaryl-substituted styrene/stilbene derivatives, expecting a domino reductive cascade involving nitroso formation, electrocyclization to a nitrone, intramolecular trapping, and hydroxylamine reduction.

outcome.observation: Hypothesis formed for TiCl3-mediated reductive aminohydroxylation.

evidence.text_span: Building on these considerations, we questioned whether a reductive cyclization strategy could bypass preformed anilides or sulfonamides and enable a unified entry to both [3,2-b]- fused products. Our previous studies established that aqueous TiCl3 (in 2 N HCl) efficiently promotes the Cadogan− 3        Sundberg reaction19,20 at room temperature (Scheme 2a),21 a protocol validated in the total synthesis of complex indole alkaloids, including (+) -1,2-dehydroaspidospermidine, (+) -condyfoline, and (−) -tubifoline.21 These precedents led us to hypothesize that a TiCl3 -mediated reductive cyclization of ortho-nitro-substituted styrene or stilbene derivative (7 and 8) might provide a unified approach to dihydrofuro[3,2- b]indoline 4 and benzofuro[3,2-b]indoline 5, respectively. The hypothetic reaction pathway is outlined in Scheme 2b. Partial reduction of nitro to nitroso group followed by a 5-center-6π- electrocyclization22 would generate nitrone intermediate 9 from precursor 7 or 8. A subsequent cyclization would furnish 10, which upon reduction of the hydroxylamine function would provide 4 or 5, depending on the nature of the tether connecting the hydroxyl group and the Csp2 carbon. However, intermediate 9 could undergo 1,2-Wagner−Meerwein rearrangement to afford either 11 (pathway b) or 12 (pathway c).21b,23 Intermediate 11 could be further reduced to the 2,3,3- trisubstituted indolenine 13, whereas 12 could evolve to 1 or 2 via a sequence of cyclization and reduction. Thus, precise control of the reaction conditions is critical to override rearrangement pathways and enforce the selective formation of the kinetically programmed [3,2-b] framework. Herein, we report that TiCl3 -mediated reductive aminohydroxylation of nitroarene derivatives 7 or 8 proceeded smoothly to afford 4 or 5 in good to excellent yields.

thought.background: The general hypothesis for TiCl3-mediated reductive aminohydroxylation of ortho-nitroaryl-substituted styrene/stilbene derivatives (7 or 8) to dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indolines (4) or benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines (5) has been established. (E)-4-(2-nitrophenyl)-3,4-diphenylbut-3-en-1-ol (7a) was chosen as the model substrate.
thought.gap: Optimal reaction conditions (solvent, additives, temperature) need to be determined to achieve selective formation of the desired [3,2-b] framework and suppress side reactions like overreduction or rearrangement.
thought.decision: To optimize the reaction, we will screen conditions using 7a as a model substrate, focusing on solvent degassing, ammonium salt addition to control acidity, and temperature to prevent overreduction. We expect to find conditions that favor the desired cyclization pathway.

outcome.observation: The desired dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indoline 4a was isolated in 59% yield under optimized conditions. Key observations included the need for degassed solvent, ammonium salt to attenuate acidity, and low temperature to suppress overreduction.

evidence.text_span: (E)-4-(2-nitrophenyl)-3,4-diphenylbut-3-en-1-ol (7a, R1Σ = R2 = Ph) was selected as the model substrate for the evaluation of reaction conditions. The following key experimental observations emerged (see the Supporting Information (SI) for details): (a) the solvent (MeCN) needed to be carefully degassed to prevent the cleavage of the double bond; (b) addition of ammonium salt to attenuate the acidity of the reaction mixture proved beneficial, with a 1:4 ratio of TiCl3 (2N in HCl) / NH4OAc identified as optimal; and (c) the reaction was best performed at −10 °C to suppress overreduction of nitroarene to the corresponding aniline.24 Overall, the optimal conditions involved slow addition of degassed aqueous TiCl3 (15.0 equiv) to a solution of 7a and N H̄4OAc (60.0 equiv) in degassed MeCN χ′c0.05M) at −10 °C, followed by stirring at the same temperature for 3 h. Under these conditions, the desired dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indoline 4a was isolated in 59% yield (Scheme 3).

thought.background: Optimized conditions for the reductive aminohydroxylation of styrene derivatives (7) have been established using 7a as a model substrate. Now, the scope of the reaction needs to be explored with various substituents.
thought.gap: Determine the compatibility of different aryl and alkyl substituents at the C3 and C4 positions of the styrene derivative, as well as substituents on the nitroarene moiety, with the optimized reaction conditions.
thought.decision: To evaluate the scope, a range of substrates bearing electron-donating, electron-withdrawing, and various alkyl groups will be synthesized (via Suzuki-Miyaura cross-coupling) and subjected to the optimized TiCl3-mediated reductive aminohydroxylation. We expect good to excellent yields for most substrates, but anticipate potential issues with ortho-substituted aryl groups or highly electron-donating groups at C3 due to competitive side reactions.

outcome.observation: A wide range of aryl substituents (electron-donating and electron-withdrawing) at C3 and C4 were well-tolerated, as were various alkyl substituents and substituents on the nitroarene moiety, generally affording good to excellent yields. Anilines from overreduction were minor byproducts. Double bond geometry had no effect.

evidence.text_span: With the optimized conditions in hand, the scope of this reaction was examined. The starting materials were prepared by Suzuki−Miyaura cross-coupling between enol tosylates and arylboronates according to Gosselin (see the SI).25 As shown in Scheme 3, a wide range of aryl substituents at the C3 and C4 positions of 4-(2-nitrophenyl)but-3-en-1-ol were welltolerated. Substrates bearing either electron-donating (Me, OMe) or electron-withdrawing groups (CO2Et, F, CF3,), at the para- or meta-position reacted smoothly to furnish the corresponding tricyclic products. In all cases, anilines arising from complete reduction of the nitro group were isolated as minor byproducts, while the double bond geometry of 7 had no discernible effect on the reaction outcome.

thought.background: The scope of the reductive aminohydroxylation has been explored for various substituents. However, some specific substituents might pose challenges.
thought.gap: Determine the effect of ortho-substituents on the aryl ring and highly electron-donating groups at the C3 position on the reaction efficiency.
thought.decision: To understand the limitations, substrates with an ortho-substituent (4l) and a 4-methoxyphenyl group at C3 (4i) will be tested under the optimized conditions. We expect diminished yields due to steric hindrance or competitive side reactions.

outcome.observation: Introduction of an ortho substituent on the aryl ring (4l) significantly diminished the yield. A similar decrease in efficiency was observed for substrate 4i (4-methoxyphenyl group at C3), presumably due to competitive side reactions (Wagner-Meerwein rearrangement).

evidence.text_span: In contrast, introduction of an ortho substituent on the aryl ring significantly diminished the yield of the desired product (4l). A similar decrease in efficiency was observed for substrate 4i, bearing a 4- methoxyphenyl group at the C3 position, presumably due to competitive side reactions outlined in Scheme 2b.

thought.background: The scope of the reductive aminohydroxylation has been successfully demonstrated for styrene derivatives (7). Now, the focus shifts to stilbene derivatives (8) to access benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines (5).
thought.gap: The optimized conditions for styrene derivatives might not be directly applicable to stilbene derivatives due to structural differences. New reaction conditions need to be optimized for stilbene derivatives.
thought.decision: To extend the methodology to stilbene derivatives, reaction conditions will be reoptimized using a model stilbene substrate. We expect to find a suitable solvent system and temperature that promotes the desired cyclization to benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines.

outcome.observation: Reoptimized conditions (TiCl3 (20.0 equiv), Me2CO-H2O (1:1), rt, 16 h) allowed stilbene derivative 8a to undergo smooth double cyclization, affording benzofuro[3,2-b]indoline 5a in 69% yield. Tetracyclic compounds 5b and 5c were also prepared in good yields.

evidence.text_span: We next turned our attention to the reductive aminohydroxylation of stilbene derivatives (Scheme 4). The reaction conditions were reoptimized and under optimized conditions [TiCl3 (20.0 equiv), Me2CO-H2O (v/v = 1:1)), rt, 16 h], 8a R1 = nBu , R2 = Ph′) underwent smooth double cyclization to afford benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines 5a in 69% yield. Tetracyclic compounds 5b and 5c were similarly prepared in yields of 89% and 73% , respectively.

thought.background: The optimized conditions for stilbene derivatives successfully yielded benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines. However, the possibility of competitive rearrangement pathways, as outlined in the initial hypothesis (Scheme 2b), remains a concern, especially with certain substituents.
thought.gap: Investigate if specific substituents on the stilbene derivative can promote the undesired Wagner-Meerwein rearrangement pathway, leading to regioisomeric products.
thought.decision: To probe the selectivity, stilbene derivative 8d, with a 4-methoxyphenyl group at the C3 position, will be reacted under the optimized conditions. We expect to observe the formation of both the desired [3,2-b] product (5d) and the rearranged [2,3-b] product (2d) due to the stability of the nitrone intermediate and the migratory aptitude of the ortho-hydroxyphenyl group.

outcome.observation: Reaction of 8d under the same conditions produced two separable compounds, 5d (42%) and 2d (40%), in approximately 1:1 ratio. Compound 2d resulted from a Wagner-Meerwein rearrangement of the nitrone intermediate 9d, followed by cyclization and reduction, indicating a significant competing pathway for this substrate.

evidence.text_span: Interestingly, reaction of 8d under the same conditions produced two separable compounds 5d (42%) and 2d (40%) , in approximately 1:1 ratio. While compound 5d was generated following the expected pathway involving the cyclization of the nitrone intermediate 9d, the formation of 2d can be rationalized by a Wagner−Meerwein rearrangement of 9d, followed by cyclization of the resulting 3,3-disubstituted indolenine N-oxide 12d and subsequent reduction (cf Scheme 2b, pathway c). The high stability of the nitrone intermediate, due to the 4-methoxyphenyl group at the C3 position of 9d, together with the high migratory aptitude of ortho-hydroxyphenyl group, renders this competing pathway significant.

thought.background: The reductive aminohydroxylation has been successfully applied to styrene and stilbene derivatives, demonstrating double cyclization. The next step is to explore the possibility of a triple cyclization.
thought.gap: Can the methodology be extended to achieve a triple cyclization, forming three rings in a single operation, which would be challenging under oxidative conditions?
thought.decision: To demonstrate the power of orchestrated multiple bond-forming processes, substrate 7z, designed for triple cyclization, will be treated with TiCl3 and NH4OAc under the established conditions. We expect the formation of a hexahydro-furo[3,2-b]pyrido[1,2-a]indole (4z).

outcome.observation: Treatment of 7z with TiCl3 and NH4OAc afforded the tetracyclic compound 4z in 51% yield. This domino transformation involved the sequential formation of two C-N bonds and one C-O bond, constructing all three rings in a single operation.

evidence.text_span: A triple cyclization was subsequently developed (Scheme 5). Treatment of an acetonitrile solution of 7z with TiCl3 and NH4OAc afforded tetracyclic compound 4z in 51% yield. This domino transformation proceeds through the sequential formation of two C−N bonds and one C- Ō bond, illustrating the power of orchestrated multiple bond-forming processes. Notably, all three rings are constructed in a single operation, highlighting the synthetic advantage of the present reductive double-cyclization protocol. Such a transformation would be unattainable under previously reported oxidative conditions, which rely on protected aniline as one of the internal nucleophiles.

thought.background: The proposed mechanism for the reductive aminohydroxylation involves a cyclic hydroxylamine intermediate (10). Direct evidence for this intermediate would strengthen the mechanistic understanding.
thought.gap: Can the hydroxylamine intermediate (10) be isolated and subsequently converted to the final product (4), providing direct support for its role in the reaction pathway?
thought.decision: To confirm the mechanistic pathway, reaction progress will be carefully monitored to isolate hydroxylamine 10y. This isolated intermediate will then be subjected to standard reduction conditions to see if it converts to 4y, thus validating its role as an intermediate.

outcome.observation: Hydroxylamine 10y was successfully isolated by carefully monitoring the reaction progress. Subsequent reduction of 10y under standard conditions proceeded smoothly to furnish 4y, supporting its role as an intermediate in the reductive aminohydroxylation process.

evidence.text_span: By carefully monitoring the reaction progress, we were able to isolate hydroxylamine 10y. Subsequent reduction of 10y under standard conditions proceeded smoothly to furnish 4y, supporting the notion that 10y served as an intermediate in the reductive aminohydroxylation process (Scheme 6a).

thought.background: The reductive aminohydroxylation successfully yields dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indolines. However, the possibility of further reduction under certain conditions needs to be investigated.
thought.gap: Determine if the dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indoline product can undergo further reduction with aqueous TiCl3, and if so, what the resulting product would be.
thought.decision: To understand the stability of the product under reductive conditions, dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indoline 4a will be treated with aqueous TiCl3 at room temperature with extended reaction time. We expect to observe further reduction to a 2,2,3-trisubstituted indoline (14).

outcome.observation: Dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indoline 4a undergoes further reduction with aqueous TiCl3 at room temperature to afford the 2,2,3-trisubstituted indoline 14. This product was also formed when the initial reductive cyclization of 7a was conducted at room temperature with extended reaction time, indicating overreduction.

evidence.text_span: An additional control experiment indicated that dihydrofuro[3,2- b]indoline 4a undergoes further reduction with aqueous TiCl3 at room temperature to afford the 2,2,3-trisubstituted indoline 14, a structural motif that, although rare, is found in indole alkaloids (Scheme 6b).26 Consistent with this observation, compound 14 was also formed when the reductive cyclization of 7a was conducted at room temperature with extended reaction time.

validation.significance: The developed TiCl3-mediated reductive cyclization provides a novel and mechanistically distinct route to dihydrofuro[3,2-b]indolines and benzofuro[3,2-b]indolines, complementing existing oxidative methods. It offers a practical approach to these tricyclic and tetracyclic frameworks, bypassing the need for nitrogen protection and enabling complex triple cyclizations unattainable by previous methods. The ability to access both product classes from a common precursor class represents a significant advance.

source.title: TiCl<sub>3</sub>-Mediated Reductive Aminohydroxylation of Alkenes to (Benzo)Furo[3,2-<i>b</i>]Indolines.
source.authors: Boyarskaya D, Gri P, Delayre B, Wang Q, Zhu J.
